key_insight: Unsupervised style-transfer domain adaptation plus a Swin Transformer V2 classifier improved calcification malignancy classification across mammography sites and vendors.
clinical_relevance: This could make mammography CAD models more robust when deployed across hospitals, imaging systems, and acquisition techniques.
method: Generated vendor- and technique-specific training samples using AdaIN and CycleGAN without extra annotations.
Trained a supervised Swin Transformer V2 classifier for benign vs malignant calcification classification.
Tested cross-validation on OPTIMAM and external validation on EMBED and Duke Calcification Dataset v1.
evidence_signal: The model improved external AUC from 0.68 to 0.72 on EMBED and from 0.68 to 0.73 on the Duke Calcification Dataset, using multi-site mammography datasets totaling 3,907 cases across evaluation sets described in the abstract.
watch_item: Performance gains were shown on limited external cohorts, so broader validation across more sites, vendors, and acquisition protocols is still needed.
paper_title: Unsupervised Domain Adaptation for Calcification Classification in Mammography Across Multi-Site Datasets
